ui.title: FAHRENHEIT 451
ui.subtitle: Terminal Text Adventure
ui.press_enter: Press ENTER to start your adventure...
ui.memory_forever: Your memory will burn forever in the minds of those who read.

intro.title:
FAHRENHEIT 451

Based on the novel by Ray Bradbury
Written by Len Neufeld and Byron Preiss
Telarium 1984

[Terminal Recreation Version]
intro.text:
The glowing salamander on your helmet burns against the dark.
You are a firefighter in a world where firemen burn books
and the population is kept docile through wall-sized television
screens. But tonight, something is different. Tonight, you
have taken a book... and you cannot burn it.

opening.text:
You are standing in your apartment. The parlour walls are alive
with mindless chatter, their giant screens flickering with
mindless entertainment. Your wife, Mildred, lies unconscious
on the bed, her nightstand table cluttered with pill bottles.

Outside your window, the city burns. The electric beetles
hum their eternal song. And in your hands, hidden in your
firefighter's jacket, is a book that you cannot bring yourself
to burn.

What will you do?

commands.try_commands: Try: LOOK, EXAMINE, INVENTORY, GO, TAKE, DROP, SPEAK, or HELP

help.title: FAHRENHEIT 451 - HELP
help.go_cmd: GO <dir>      - Walk in a direction (NORTH, SOUTH, EAST, WEST)
help.move_cmd: N, S, E, W    - Quick movement
help.tip2: - Objects may be used in unexpected ways
help.tip4: - Remember: books are dangerous in this world

endings.death_1:
The Mechanical Hound's needle-tongue strikes your neck.
The poison races through your veins. Darkness takes you.

You are dead.

The firemen will burn your house tomorrow. Your wife will
forget you ever existed. And the books... the books will
burn forever.
endings.death_2:
The Hound Patrol has found you. Before you can react,
a squadron of firemen storm the building. You are
surrounded, arrested, and taken to the detention center.

The trials are brief. The verdict is always the same.
You are sentenced to death by burning.
endings.escape:
You climb aboard the speeding subway train as it plunges
into the darkness. The rails sing beneath your feet.
And then, slowly, the train rises above the city.

There, on the outskirts, you see them: the book people.
Men and women who have memorized the greatest works of
literature, who have become living books. They welcome you
among them.

Clarisse is there, smiling. "We are the salt of the earth,"
she says. "We are the ones who remember."

You have escaped the burning world.
endings.failure:
You tried to save the books, but in the end, they burned
all the same. The fire department has been expanded.
More Hounds patrol the streets. More citizens are
arrested and burned.

The world grows darker. And the flames never stop.
